name: linkedin-automation
description: Automate LinkedIn tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): create posts, manage profile, company info, comments, and image uploads. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Create and publish LinkedIn text posts with visibility controls
Retrieve authenticated user profile information and company details
Upload and attach images to LinkedIn posts
Manage LinkedIn OAuth connections and authentication
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 44 static analysis findings are false positives from markdown documentation. The external_commands flags are backtick characters in code blocks, network finding is legitimate documentation of MCP endpoint, weak_crypto flags are false matches on tool names, and system_reconnaissance flags are documentation of LinkedIn API URN formats. No executable code present. This is safe documentation for LinkedIn API workflows.
valueStatement: Save time by automating LinkedIn post creation, profile management, and engagement tasks through Composio's LinkedIn toolkit. Streamline social media workflows without leaving your development environment.
userTitle: Automate LinkedIn Posts and Profile Management
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active LinkedIn OAuth authentication
Can only manage posts and profile for the authenticated user, not other users
Subject to LinkedIn API rate limits and daily posting quotas
Image uploads require two-step process: register upload then upload binary
Cannot create polls or documents without additional LinkedIn API features

useCases.title: Scheduled Content Publishing
useCases.description: Marketing professionals automate LinkedIn post creation and publishing as part of content workflows, maintaining consistent social media presence without manual intervention.

useCases.title: Company Profile Management
useCases.description: Social media managers retrieve and update company page information, post updates on behalf of organization pages, and manage engagement from within Claude Code.

useCases.title: Developer Integration
useCases.description: Developers integrate LinkedIn posting capabilities into custom automation pipelines, triggering social media updates based on events or deployments.

promptTemplates.title: Create a simple LinkedIn post
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a LinkedIn post with the text: 'Excited to share our latest product update! Check out our new features.' Make it visible to my connections.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner user wants to post a simple text update to LinkedIn

promptTemplates.title: Post with image attachment
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me post to LinkedIn with an image. The text should be 'Behind the scenes at our team meetup!' and the image is at /path/to/team-photo.jpg. Make it public.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to create a LinkedIn post with an attached image from their local filesystem

promptTemplates.title: Retrieve profile and company information
promptTemplates.prompt: Get my LinkedIn profile information and also fetch details for company ID 12345678. Show me the key details from both.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to retrieve their personal profile and specific company page information

promptTemplates.title: Comment on a post by ID
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a comment 'Great insights! Thanks for sharing.' to LinkedIn post with ID 'urn:li:activity:1234567890'. My URN is 'urn:li:person:ABC123'.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced user wants to programmatically comment on a specific LinkedIn post using URN identifiers

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to retrieve the current tool schemas before using any LinkedIn operations
Verify LinkedIn connection status is ACTIVE before attempting any operations by calling RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS
Use explicit visibility settings ('PUBLIC' or 'CONNECTIONS') rather than relying on defaults to ensure posts reach intended audience
Implement backoff strategies when creating multiple posts or comments to respect LinkedIn API rate limits and avoid 429 errors
antiPatterns: Do not skip the RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS step as tool schemas may change and using outdated parameters will cause operations to fail
Do not use partial IDs or vanity URLs instead of full URN formats when referencing posts, users, or organizations
Do not create posts or comments in rapid succession without delays as this will trigger LinkedIn rate limits and may result in temporary throttling
Do not assume OAuth tokens are valid indefinitely; re-authenticate if API calls return 401 errors indicating expired credentials

faq.question: Do I need a LinkedIn API key?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses Rube MCP which handles LinkedIn OAuth authentication. You just need to authenticate via the RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS workflow.

faq.question: Can I post on behalf of other users?
faq.answer: No. This skill can only post and manage content for the authenticated user account or company pages where you are an admin.

faq.question: Why did my post creation fail with a URN error?
faq.answer: You must call LINKEDIN_GET_MY_INFO first to retrieve your profile URN, then use that URN as the actor parameter when creating posts or comments.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: LinkedIn typically supports JPG, PNG, and GIF formats. Images must be uploaded via the two-step process: register upload, then upload binary data.

faq.question: How do I avoid rate limiting issues?
faq.answer: Implement delays between posts, monitor for 429 status codes, and respect the Retry-After header. LinkedIn has strict daily limits on posts and comments.

faq.question: Can I schedule posts for future publication?
faq.answer: No. The LinkedIn API does not support scheduled posting. Posts are published immediately upon creation. Use external scheduling tools if needed.
